29.summary: Based on 215 useful public comments. Of 75 comments with detectable reaction wording, 63 were appreciative and 12 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, acting & characters, emotion.

Anthony.summary: Based on 5 useful public comments. Too few comments contain clear reaction wording to describe an audience balance reliably. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss direction & making.

Blast.summary: Based on 702 useful public comments. Of 224 comments with detectable reaction wording, 195 were appreciative and 29 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, story & screenplay, acting & characters.

Double Occupancy.summary: Based on 9 useful public comments. Too few comments contain clear reaction wording to describe an audience balance reliably. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss story & screenplay.

Gandhi Talks.summary: Based on 289 useful public comments. Of 64 comments with detectable reaction wording, 44 were appreciative and 20 were critical; appreciative and critical signals are mixed. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, music & sound, story & screenplay.

Gatta Kusthi 2.summary: Based on 442 useful public comments. Of 120 comments with detectable reaction wording, 96 were appreciative and 24 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, comedy, acting & characters.

Granny.summary: Based on 16 useful public comments. Too few comments contain clear reaction wording to describe an audience balance reliably. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss pacing & runtime.

Happy Raj.summary: Based on 8 useful public comments. Too few comments contain clear reaction wording to describe an audience balance reliably. Most collected comments are general reactions rather than discussion of a specific film aspect.

Idhayam Murali.summary: Based on 593 useful public comments. Of 217 comments with detectable reaction wording, 153 were appreciative and 64 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, acting & characters, emotion.

Kara.summary: Based on 807 useful public comments. Of 259 comments with detectable reaction wording, 167 were appreciative and 92 were critical; appreciative and critical signals are mixed. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss story & screenplay, release & theatre, acting & characters.

Karuppu.summary: Based on 2,394 useful public comments. Of 638 comments with detectable reaction wording, 467 were appreciative and 171 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, story & screenplay, music & sound.

Mr. X.summary: Based on 10 useful public comments. Too few comments contain clear reaction wording to describe an audience balance reliably. Most collected comments are general reactions rather than discussion of a specific film aspect.

Parimala and Co.summary: Based on 442 useful public comments. Of 124 comments with detectable reaction wording, 85 were appreciative and 39 were critical; appreciative and critical signals are mixed. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss comedy, story & screenplay, release & theatre.

Thaai Kizhavi.summary: Based on 862 useful public comments. Of 242 comments with detectable reaction wording, 205 were appreciative and 37 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, acting & characters, story & screenplay.

Youth.summary: Based on 1,092 useful public comments. Of 305 comments with detectable reaction wording, 229 were appreciative and 76 were critical; appreciative signals clearly outweigh critical signals. Beyond general reactions, viewers most often discuss release & theatre, acting & characters, story & screenplay.

insight_method: Rule-based language, topic, depth and reaction-signal aggregation; no review score inferred

movie_catalog.title: Idhayam Murali
movie_catalog.overview: A young man navigates the complexities of unrequited love, finding that even as years pass and life takes unexpected turns, certain emotions remain deeply embedded in his heart.

movie_catalog.title: Karuppu
movie_catalog.overview: In a world where justice falters, guardian deity Vettai Karuppu takes the guise of a lawyer to battle a corrupt legal system preying on the powerless.

movie_catalog.title: Parimala and Co
movie_catalog.overview: A dark comedy centered around a chaotic family whose tangled relationships and unpredictable situations lead to emotionally charged and absurd moments, blending humor with underlying tension and drama.

movie_catalog.title: Blast
movie_catalog.overview: A seemingly ordinary family must unleash their hidden martial arts skills when they clash with a ruthless criminal gang tied to corruption and violence.

movie_catalog.title: Kara
movie_catalog.overview: A thief tries to go straight, but when predatory banks trap his father in debt, he returns to crime — with a determined cop closing in on his trail.

movie_catalog.title: Gatta Kusthi 2
movie_catalog.overview: Picks up after the first film, with Veera and Keerthi balancing parenthood and Keerthi's wrestling career, featuring a role reversal where Veera takes on more domestic responsibilities.

movie_catalog.title: With Love
movie_catalog.overview: Sathya reluctantly agrees to a blind date arranged by his sister and meets Monisha. They discover they attended the same school years ago. Bonding over shared memories and old crushes, they reconnect with their past and grow closer.

movie_catalog.title: Thaai Kizhavi
movie_catalog.overview: An elderly and feared moneylender from a village near Madurai falls seriously ill, prompting her estranged sons and extended family to reunite after years of separation. As tensions rise inside the household, hidden motives, family conflicts, and expectations over her wealth turn her uncertain condition into the center of a chaotic family drama.

movie_catalog.title: Gandhi Talks
movie_catalog.overview: A silent black comedy, about the monetary needs of a character & how it impacts the others. A young, unemployed graduate Mahadev’s struggle to land a job through any means possible and crosses paths with a businessman and petty thief. A subject wherein silence speaks much louder than words. Although a work of fiction by the writer, all the characters in the film are sketched out to seem very real and relatable ensuring an enriching journey as well a laugh riot as the cat and mouse guffaws amongst them unfold. Gandhi Talks aims at telling a story by switching off the device of dialogue, which is not only scary but also interesting and challenging.

movie_catalog.title: Youth
movie_catalog.overview: Youth follows Praveen, a 15-year-old boy who enters adolescence determined to find true love before school ends. As he goes through multiple relationships and heartbreaks, he slowly begins to understand what love truly means. Along the way, the experiences shape his growth, maturity, and outlook on life.

A quirky 29-year-old named Sathya embarks on an unexpected journey of laughter, unlikely friendships, and self-discovery. Through hilarious misadventures and chance encounters, he slowly sees life's bigger picture - its surprises, joys, and second chances.

movie_catalog.title: TN 2026
movie_catalog.overview: Set in Pollachi, the story follows a powerful landlord, Sivalingam Mandradiyar, who becomes obsessed with a rising actor, Kukanth Kumar, believing he is the next M.G.R. He pours his wealth into shaping the actor's career and political future. But as ambition and fame grow, so does the risk of downfall. Will it lead to power or ruin?

movie_catalog.title: Mr. X
movie_catalog.overview: Centers on secret intelligence agency cases involving five major national threats that were thwarted through covert operations.

movie_catalog.title: Granny
movie_catalog.overview: Granny is an upcoming film directed by Vijaya Kumara, starring Vadivukkarasi, Dhileepan, Singampuli, Gaja Raja, Ananthu Nag, and Aparna in prominent roles.

movie_catalog.title: Arjunan Per Paththu
movie_catalog.overview: Arjunan, who witnessed his parents being killed by lightning in front of his eyes, cannot bear the shock and arrives in Chennai from his hometown Madurai. Arjunan, who joined a kayalang shop in Chennai, starts to love a woman. Before marrying that woman, he was a worker and wanted to become an entrepreneur. He gave away all the money he had saved up until then and bought an old Tata Ace and married the woman he loved. Arjuna happily starts driving the vehicle, thinking that his life has become as happy as he thought it would be. Arjuna, who has been facing constant problems with that old vehicle, realizes that he has been completely cheated in buying the vehicle. He brings together all those who have been affected like him and exposes all the frauds in buying and selling old vehicles, seeking justice for himself and all those who have been cheated like him.

movie_catalog.title: Double Occupancy
movie_catalog.overview: Rajini transforms between woman by day and man by night, hiding this gender swap from partners creating tangled complications.

movie_catalog.title: Neelira
movie_catalog.overview: A wedding eve in 1988 Sri Lanka turns into a hostage standoff when Indian Army soldiers occupy a family's home overnight.

movie_catalog.title: Happy Raj
movie_catalog.overview: A city romance meets a rural family invasion. What follows is chaos, comedy, and heartfelt moments. The story unfolds as a joyful entertainer that explores love, cultural clashes, and the importance of family bonds.

movie_catalog.title: Find Me
movie_catalog.overview: During the COVID-19 lockdown, a series of mysterious murders spreads fear across the city. As the investigation unfolds, hidden secrets and unexpected twists make it increasingly difficult to identify the killer. Every clue leads to another mystery in this Tamil crime thriller.

movie_catalog.title: Anthony
movie_catalog.overview: Set along the coastal shores of Jaffna, the story follows a fisherman and his family  whose lives are inseparably tied to the sea. As he struggles to balance his responsibilities toward his profession and his family, a tender love story unfolds, shaping his choices and testing his values. Daily life within the fishing community  its hardships, traditions, and unspoken bonds, forms the backdrop of the narrative. When personal desires begin to clash with family expectations, the protagonist is forced to confront sacrifice, loyalty, and the true meaning of love. Through moments of romance, conflict, and emotional depth, the film reveals the  strength of family ties and the resilience of a community that survives by trusting the sea and each other.
